signal: Monolithic 4207-line module with 66 functions, 10 global variables, eval() usage, and 22 generic Exception handlers—lacks modularity, untestable subsystems, error semantics unclear.

Five separate cloner classes (element_cloner, file_based_element_cloner, cdp_element_cloner, comprehensive_element_cloner, progressive_element_cloner) with overlapping semantics—duplicated logic, maintenance burden, inconsistent behavior.

1335-line resource manager with 28 async methods, 90 process/PID references, but only 1 error-handling pattern in lifecycle and no context manager support—resource leak risks, hard to guarantee cleanup.
Version-aware singleton with 8 global variables and async/concurrency patterns but no thread-safety synchronization—multi-version backend state collision risk under concurrent access.
get_hook_examples() function spans 309 lines in monolithic implementation—massive single function with unclear flow, hard to unit test or modify.
841-line script execution engine with getattr() patterns and dynamic code paths—potential injection attack surface, requires security audit.
4 classes, dynamic code execution via exec/eval patterns, 15 try/15 except pairs—security audit gaps, runtime injection risks, error handling unclear.
1023-line process manager with 29 functions, 30 exception handlers, and 2 large functions (94 and 74 lines)—error handling patterns unclear, hard to diagnose cleanup failures.
DOM operations with 9 hardcoded timeouts/sleeps and 3 silent except-pass patterns—flaky integration tests, hidden failures on slow systems or network latency.
606-line single class with monolithic function, 14 try/16 except blocks—unclear error recovery semantics, network failure handling fragile.
32 test files across 30+ embedded modules (most modules have only 1 test file)—critical subsystems under-tested; server.py, process_cleanup, browser_manager lack comprehensive test suites.
CLI argument handling has 11 help strings across 0 subcommands with no error recovery try/except—poor user experience on malformed args or failures.
State persistence module lacks version isolation boundaries—multi-version backend may read/write incompatible state formats.
Only 2 workflow files (test.yml, publish.yml)—limited platform/environment coverage, no per-PR code quality gates beyond basic test pass.
